nodes.label: Active Initiative Workflow
nodes.rationale: Analyze documented and implemented business logic, retain the analysis locally, then limit subsequent changes to fixes identified by that analysis.

nodes.label: Single-Parameter Skill Name Refactor
nodes.rationale: Namespace should be used only as a GitHub API owner/repository parameter; after fetching, the combined owner/repository or skill path is a single skill name.

nodes.label: PRD-Compliant Git Commit Format
nodes.rationale: Git commit messages must conform to the PRD convention.

nodes.label: Filesystem as Source of Truth
nodes.rationale: The directory tree is authoritative; manifest.yaml is the skill-level source, skills-lock.json mirrors it, and writes update the manifest before the lock.

nodes.label: Core and CLI Separation
nodes.rationale: Core is pure TypeScript using SkillSyncContext dependency injection, while CLI and HTTP layers remain thin wrappers.

nodes.label: Isolated Skill Test Environment
nodes.rationale: Tests must never use real user skill directories and instead redirect central and Agent paths with SKILL_SYNC_HOME and SKILL_SYNC_AGENTS_DIR.

nodes.label: Document and Implementation Gaps
nodes.rationale: The report identifies documented requirements that differ from behavior, prioritizing undeploy preservation, security checks, data completeness, backups, and distribution behavior.

nodes.label: Simplified Version Management
nodes.rationale: Maintain one active version, automatically back up before upgrades, and restore from backups rather than support concurrent versions.

nodes.label: SkillSyncContext Dependency Injection
nodes.rationale: Core modules receive runtime paths, configuration, and lock-file context without depending on a CLI or HTTP framework.

nodes.label: Platform Link Strategy
nodes.rationale: macOS and Linux prefer symlinks, while Windows prefers junctions because they do not need administrator or developer-mode privileges; copy is the fallback.

nodes.label: Undeploy Preserved Copy
nodes.rationale: Undeploying a link replaces it with a local copy and marks it unmanaged so it is retained but no longer updated.

nodes.label: Repository Inference from skills.sh Results
nodes.rationale: Because skills.sh results do not provide a repository name, try the skill ID as the repository and then search the owner's repositories with the GitHub Trees API.

nodes.label: Secrets Separation
nodes.rationale: Tokens and passwords stay in ignored secrets.yaml and are referenced from config.yaml rather than synchronized.

nodes.label: Skill Dependency Management
nodes.rationale: Dependencies use SemVer expressions and recursive cycle checks but remain warning-level because SkillSync is not intended to be a package manager.

nodes.label: Lazy GitHub Token Strategy
nodes.rationale: Do not proactively obtain a GitHub token; attempt configured or authenticated tokens only after rate limiting.

nodes.label: Layered Test Suite
nodes.rationale: Use mandatory unit and integration tests plus optional E2E tests, with mocks restricted to HTTP and Git boundaries.

nodes.label: Backup Retention Policy
nodes.rationale: Keep the five most recent backups by default, automatically delete oldest excess backups, and permit manual cleanup.

nodes.label: Namespace Collision Decision
nodes.rationale: Namespaces distinguish identically named skills from separate sources in the central skills directory.

nodes.label: Web UI Framework Decision
nodes.rationale: Vue 3, Naive UI, Vite, UnoCSS, and md-editor-v3 provide a TypeScript-friendly UI with component coverage and safe Markdown rendering without rebuilding these capabilities.
